title: CRISPRi-mediated transcriptional silencing in iPSCs for the study of human brain development
author: Johansson, Adami, et al.
description: SummaryThis protocol describes the design and use of CRISPRi-mediated transcriptional silencing in human iPSCs, for loss-of-function studies in brain development research. The protocol avoids single cell selection, thereby eliminating side effects of clonal expansion and sites of viral integration. We also describe a neural progenitor differentiation protocol and discuss the challenges of target-specific lentiviral silencing, efficient silencing levels, and off-target effects.For complete details on the use and execution of this protocol, please refer to Johansson et al. (2022).

steps_raw.title: Before you begin
steps_raw.instruction: The protocol below describes the specific steps for using CRISPRi in human induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs). This protocol has also been used in fibroblasts converted to induced neurons (Pircs et al., 2022) and is likely suitable for other cell lines. We have used an all-in-one CRISPRi backbone from Addgene (Thakore et al., 2015; Addgene_71237). Molecular cloning, lentiviral production, qRT-PCR and hiPSCs culture techniques need to be established in the lab for successful application of this protocol (these techniques are not covered here). Verified qRT-PCR primers for the gene of interest and one or two housekeeping genes are also essential.

steps_raw.title: Combine guides for analysis for reduction of off-target effects
Combine guides for analysis for reduction of off-target effects
steps_raw.instruction: This part of the protocol involves combining or verifying the effect of the CRISPRi using two different guides. This varies depending on the read-out. RNA-sequencing is used here as readout, and the combination of guides will be described below (Figure 7).Figure 7Schematics of fbNPCs FACS and RNA-seq analysesOnce the GFP+ fbNPCs are sorted via FACS, they are prepared for RNA-sequencing. The sequencing results are then analyzed to compare transcriptional differences between the control cells and the CRISPRi cells.

steps_raw.title: Combine guides for analysis for reduction of off-target effects
steps_raw.instruction: Most guides will have off-targets. However, two guides are very unlikely to have the same off-targets. Thus, combining the readouts of two guides will minimize the off-target effect of each guide. Here, we combine the two CRISPRi conditions into one, and analysis is performed with control vs CRISPRi cells (in our case n=4 and 8, respectively).
